translations.descricao: Allergic rhinitis is an IgE-mediated inflammatory disease of the nasal mucosa, characterized by symptoms such as sneezing, rhinorrhea, nasal obstruction, and itching. The condition affects quality of life and may be associated with asthma. In the Brazilian context, it represents a significant challenge in the public health system.
translations.sus.indicacao: Recommended diagnosis and management for patients with persistent or intermittent nasal symptoms, especially in populations with a family history of atopy or exposure to allergens.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Individuals of all ages with symptoms suggestive of rhinitis, prioritizing children and adults in urban areas with high environmental exposure.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: The ARIA (Allergic Rhinitis and its Impact on Asthma) 2019 recommends evaluation for patients with allergic nasal symptoms, integrating classification into intermittent/persistent and mild/moderate-severe.
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: The global prevalence of allergic rhinitis is estimated at 10-40% in adults and up to 40% in children. In Brazil, it affects about 20-30% of the population, with greater impact in urban regions.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: The annual incidence varies from 5-10% in atopic populations. In Brazil, an increase of 2-5% per year is observed in metropolitan areas.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: Direct mortality is low, less than 0.1%, but it indirectly contributes to morbidity and mortality due to respiratory complications.
